name: Baked Squid with Garlic-Anchovy Pasta - F&w 12/94
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 pounds Small squid; cleaned, bodies into 1/4-inch rings -- tentacles halved if
1 cup Plain dried bread crumbs (about 4 ounces)
1/2 pound Dried capellini (or other, thin pasta)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 450°F. Bring a large saucepan of water to a boil.
2. In a large bowl, toss the squid with the breadcrumbs, oregano, pepper, and salt. Spread the squid in a large baking dish in a single layer and sprinkle with any leftover crumbs. Drizzle 1/4 cup plus 2 tablespoons of the olive oil on top. Bake for 10 minutes, or until the squid is golden brown and crunchy.
3. Meanwhile, in a small saucepan, whisk the garlic and the anchovy paste into the remaining 1/4 cup oil and bring to a simmer over low heat. Cook, whisking, until fragrant but not browned, about 3 minutes.
4. Add salt to the boiling water. Add the capellini and cook, stirring occasionally, until al dente, about 3 minutes. Drain the pasta and return it to the saucepan. Add the anchovy sauce and the butter and toss to coat.
5. Make a bed of capellini on a platter or plates. Mound the baked squid on the pasta and serve with lemon wedges.
